name: iso-13485-certification
description: Comprehensive toolkit for preparing ISO 13485 certification documentation for medical device Quality Management Systems. Use when users need help with ISO 13485 QMS documentation, including (1) conducting gap analysis of existing documentation, (2) creating Quality Manuals, (3) developing required procedures and work instructions, (4) preparing Medical Device Files, (5) understanding ISO 13485 requirements, or (6) identifying missing documentation for medical device certification. Also use when users mention medical device regulations, QMS certification, FDA QMSR, EU MDR, or need help with quality system documentation.
capabilities: Analyzes existing documentation to identify ISO 13485 compliance gaps
Provides templates for Quality Manual and required QMS procedures
Creates Medical Device Files for regulatory compliance
Generates prioritized action plans for certification readiness
Supports FDA QMSR and EU MDR harmonization requirements
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: All 416 static findings are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted documentation keywords in markdown templates as security issues (e.g., 'SAM' as Windows Security Accounts Manager, cryptographic terms in QMS documentation). This is a legitimate ISO 13485 documentation toolkit containing templates and a local Python analysis script with no network access or credential handling.
valueStatement: Medical device companies need comprehensive ISO 13485 documentation for quality management certification. This skill provides templates, gap analysis tools, and step-by-step guidance to create all required QMS documents efficiently.
limitations: Does not replace formal ISO 13485 certification body assessment
Cannot verify actual implementation of quality processes
Does not provide legal or regulatory compliance guarantees
Templates require customization for specific organizational needs

useCases.title: Build QMS from scratch
useCases.description: New medical device companies creating their first ISO 13485 quality management system documentation package

useCases.title: Conduct gap analysis
useCases.description: Quality professionals assessing existing documentation against ISO 13485 requirements before certification audit

useCases.title: Update for QMSR transition
useCases.description: Teams updating FDA QSR documentation to align with new QMSR harmonization requirements

promptTemplates.title: Getting started
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me understand ISO 13485 requirements and where to start with documentation for our medical device company

promptTemplates.title: Gap analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze our QMS documentation and identify gaps against ISO 13485 requirements

promptTemplates.title: Create document
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) procedure for our quality management system

promptTemplates.title: Audit preparation
promptTemplates.prompt: Prepare our documentation for ISO 13485 certification audit and identify any final gaps

bestPractices: Start with policy-level documents (Quality Manual, Quality Policy) before creating detailed procedures
Cross-reference related documents to ensure consistency across your QMS documentation
Prioritize critical documents first: CAPA, Complaint Handling, Internal Audit, and Management Review
antiPatterns: Copying ISO 13485 standard text verbatim instead of describing your actual processes
Creating overly detailed procedures that belong in work instructions instead
Neglecting to maintain records that demonstrate QMS implementation

faq.question: How long does ISO 13485 certification take?
faq.answer: Full implementation typically requires 6-12 months depending on existing quality practices and organizational complexity.

faq.question: Can exclusions reduce documentation requirements?
faq.answer: Only design and development can be excluded if you are a contract manufacturer. All exclusions must be justified in the Quality Manual.

faq.question: What is a Medical Device File?
faq.answer: A consolidated file containing all documentation about a specific medical device type, replacing separate DHF, DMR, and DHR structures.

faq.question: How often are internal audits required?
faq.answer: ISO 13485 requires a documented audit program with planned intervals. Annual audits are typical but higher-risk processes need more frequent review.

faq.question: What records must be retained?
faq.answer: Records must be retained for the lifetime of the medical device plus specified periods, typically 5-10 years after discontinuation.

faq.question: Does this skill provide legal compliance advice?
faq.answer: No. This tool provides documentation guidance. Consult regulatory experts for legal compliance questions specific to your jurisdiction.
